visual_attributes.morphology: Long, narrow snout with a distinct boss at the end in males, elongated body with webbed feet
distinctive_features: Distinct boss on male snouts resembling an earthenware pot
visual_attributes.texture: Scaly with bony scutes on the back
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Females: 2.6 to 4.5 m, Males: 3 to 6 m
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Four transverse rows of two scales on the neck, continuous plate of scutes on the back

domain_specific_visual.SNOUT_LENGTH: 3.5 times longer than the width of the skull's base
domain_specific_visual.TEETH_ARRANGEMENT: 27 to 29 upper teeth and 25 or 26 lower teeth on each side

domain_specific_functional.REPRODUCTION: Females lay 20–95 eggs and guard nests
domain_specific_functional.HABITAT: Inhabits rivers in the northern Indian subcontinent

relationships.description: The gharial is native to rivers in this region

contextual_attributes.historical_period: Fossil records from Pliocene to Early Pleistocene
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Rivers in the plains of northern Indian subcontinent
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Regarded as the vehicle of the river deity Gaṅgā in Hindu culture
